use_split.rationale: Total programme anchored to [max_bvo_ontwikkelpotentie] of 589,389 m² BVO. Non-residential uses fixed by document targets: commercial [totaal_bvo_commercieel] 3,025 m², bedrijven [min_bvo_bedrijven] ≥15,000 m², kantoren [bvo_kantoren] 3,500–6,500 m² (midpoint 5,000), cultureel [bvo_cultuur_maatschappelijk] 1,000 m², social/education (basisschool 4,230 + medisch 2,200 + cultuur excluded + horeca counted in retail) - using subset of [totaal_bvo_maatschappelijk] 22,150 m² but excluding outdoor spaces (schoolplein 2,300 + buitenruimte VO 2,100) and VO school (8,320 m²) booked separately under social. Residential is the residual to reach 589,389 m² BVO, consistent with [max_woningen_nieuw_legmeer] 4,400 woningen.

reasons: Document specifies hard caps and per-use BVO targets
Residential derived as residual is consistent with 4,400 woning cap at ~125 m² gross per woning including circulation/common

unit_mix.rationale: Sociale huur 20% per [aandeel_sociaal_woningen]; smaller 1br slice for single-person households (CBS buurt avg household size 1.3).

reasons: Tenure share is explicit in document
Typology split is designer judgment informed by CBS

unit_mix.rationale: Remainder of 20% sociale huur as family-oriented 2br to support mixed demographic; complements [min_dwelling_size_private_outdoor] requiring outdoor space >50 m².
Middenhuur 40% per [aandeel_middelduur_woningen]; bulk allocated to 2br couples/small households.

reasons: Typology consistent with mainstream middenhuur stock

unit_mix.rationale: Family-suitable 3br segment within middenhuur to support family living in the Amstelveense context.

reasons: Tenure share explicit; typology split is designer judgment

unit_mix.rationale: Vrije sector 40% per [aandeel_vrije_sector_woningen]; half allocated as 2br, half as 3br/larger to capture mixed market segment.

Other half of vrije sector envelope as koop (grondgebonden/appartement), addressing family demand and aligning with Amstelveen ownership profile; supports grondgebonden woningen referenced in [max_height_rear_garden_fence].

reasons: Split between koop and vrije huur within the 40% vrije sector slice is designer judgment

reasoning_trace.decision: Adopt total programme of 589,389 m² BVO and 4,400 woningen as the upper-bound proposal, since these are explicit hard caps for the entire plangebied.
Acknowledge 3D BAG unavailable (HTTP 400). Height context derived from 2D BAG context and document constraints only — Amstelveense hoogte = 6 bouwlagen base with 12–14 bouwlagen hoogbouwaccenten.

Allocate non-residential BVO to document-specified minima: 3,025 m² retail/horeca, 15,000 m² productive (bedrijven), 5,000 m² office (midpoint of 3,500–6,500), 1,000 m² cultural, ~10,150 m² social (basisschool 4,230 + medisch 2,200 + VO 8,320 partial — capped at 10,150 to fit envelope alongside cultureel; outdoor schoolpleinen excluded from BVO).

Residential GFA set as residual to 589,389 m² → ~555,214 m². At 4,400 woningen this yields ~126 m² gross per woning (including circulation/installations), consistent with Dutch BVO/GBO ratios of ~0.78 giving ~98 m² GBO average — realistic for the 20/40/40 tenure mix.
reasoning_trace.evidence: Derived from [max_bvo_ontwikkelpotentie] minus non-residential uses; cross-checked against [max_woningen_nieuw_legmeer]

reasoning_trace.decision: Apply tenure split 20% sociale / 40% middenhuur / 40% vrije sector strictly per document. Within vrije sector, split 50/50 between vrije sector huur and koop based on designer judgment given Amstelveen market profile.
reasoning_trace.evidence: [aandeel_sociaal_woningen], [aandeel_middelduur_woningen], [aandeel_vrije_sector_woningen]; designer judgment for huur/koop split within vrije sector

reasoning_trace.decision: Bias typology toward small-to-mid units (1br/2br dominant) reflecting buurt demographics: CBS shows avg household size 1.3 in BU03621104, indicating predominantly single/couple households. Reserve ~35% for 3br/family units to support the goal of a mixed neighbourhood with grondgebonden woningen.
reasoning_trace.evidence: [cbs_demographics: 165 households, 230 population, avg HH size 1.3]; [max_height_rear_garden_fence] implies grondgebonden woningen present

reasoning_trace.decision: Estimate parking demand at ~1.0 space per woning average (4,400 spaces) plus marginal additions for commercial/office, justified by Amstelveen suburban context. Note: OSM shows tram 309 m and bus 249 m away — moderate transit; no aggressive reduction warranted. Parking solved partly via parkeergebouw with [max_parking_layers]=5 and pre-investment of €20M ([parking_garage_preinvestment]) flagged.
reasoning_trace.evidence: [parking_garage_preinvestment], [max_parking_layers]; [osm: tram 309m, bus 249m]; designer judgment on per-woning norm (document does not specify residential parking norm)

reasoning_trace.decision: Confirm green/unbuilt envelope compatibility: 70/30 bebouwd/onbebouwd per cluster ([bebouwd_onbebouwd_ratio]) and the urban intent DOEL 1 of green binnenhoven mean realistic GFA stays at — not below — the 589,389 m² envelope only if hoogbouwaccenten (12–14 bouwlagen) are used; otherwise the realistic value drops toward 500,000 m². Range 500,000–589,389 m² reflects this dependency.

reasons: Total GFA and woning cap are explicit hard constraints
Non-residential BVO values are explicitly tabulated in the stedenbouwkundig plan
Typology mix and parking norms rest on designer judgment given absence of explicit residential parking norm and unit-size policy
3D BAG unavailable — height context limited to document constraints
